lab.title: Implementing the Continuity Equation in OpenFOAM

cfd: Implement the compressible continuity equation in OpenFOAM
Understand where expansion terms appear (pressure equation)
cpp: Use namespace fvm for implicit operators
Include proper headers: fvm.H, fvc.H, fvModels.H

steps.title: Analyze rhoEqn.H from OpenFOAM Source
steps.description: Locate and study the actual continuity equation implementation
steps.cpp_concept: Reading source code, header file organization

steps.title: Create Custom Continuity Equation File
steps.description: Implement continuity matching rhoEqn.H structure: fvm::ddt(rho) + fvc::div(phi) == source

steps.title: Integrate into Main Solver Time Loop
Compile and Run Test Case
steps.description: Build solver with wmake, run forwardStep tutorial

challenges.title: Challenge 1: Incompressible Continuity Checker
challenges.description: Create a tool that monitors continuity error for incompressible solvers
hints: For incompressible: contErr = fvc::div(phi) should be ~0
Use continuityErrs.H from incompressible/cfdTools as reference
Report sumLocal, global, and cumulative errors
challenges.deliverable: Function that prints continuity error statistics

challenges.title: Challenge 2: Phase Change Expansion Term Location
challenges.description: Research and explain WHY expansion goes in pressure equation, not continuity
hints: Review mathematical derivation from Day 01 theory
For incompressible solver, pressure Poisson equation enforces div(U)=S
Search OpenFOAM solvers for 'phaseChange' or 'boiling' examples
challenges.theory_reference: Day 01, Section 1.3.3: Case 3 - Phase Change with Mass Transfer

challenges.title: Challenge 3: Custom Source Term Implementation
challenges.description: Implement a spatially-varying source term S_rho(x,y,z)
hints: Create a volScalarField for the source
Use coordinate system: mesh.C() to get cell centers
Source term can be function of position
Add to rhoEqn using fvm::Sp(source, rho) for implicit

common_errors.error: Compilation error: 'fvm' has not been declared

common_errors.cause: Time step too large for explicit fvc::div(phi)
common_errors.fix: Reduce maxCo (Courant number) in controlDict, or use implicit scheme

common_errors.cause: Sign error in source term or incorrect flux calculation
common_errors.fix: Verify phi == rho * U * Sf (mass flux, not volume flux)

common_errors.error: Link error: undefined reference to vtable
common_errors.cause: Missing virtual function implementation or library linkage
common_errors.fix: Check Make/options for -lfiniteVolume and other libraries

performance_exercise.title: Profile Your Solver
performance_exercise.target: Continuity solve should be < 10% of total runtime

part5_analysis.title: Results Analysis
tasks: Plot continuity error vs time from log file
Compare mass at inlet vs outlet (should be equal for steady state)
Verify Courant number stays below maxCo
Document: Did solver converge? Final residuals?

validation.convergence: Initial residuals decrease 3+ orders of magnitude
validation.stability: No negative densities anywhere in domain

concept_1.title: Template Classes: fvMatrix<Type>
concept_1.description: OpenFOAM uses templates to handle scalar, vector, tensor fields uniformly
concept_1.key_insight: fvScalarMatrix is a typedef: fvMatrix<scalar>. The template parameter Type determines field type.
concept_1.lab_relevance: Understanding why rhoEqn is fvScalarMatrix (rho is scalar field)

concept_2.title: The tmp<> Smart Pointer
concept_2.description: Reference-counted temporary object management prevents memory leaks
concept_2.key_insight: tmp<> auto-deletes when refcount reaches zero. Enables expression templates.
concept_2.lab_relevance: Why we can write: fvScalarMatrix rhoEqn(fvm::ddt(rho) + fvc::div(phi));

concept_3.title: Namespace fvm vs fvc
concept_3.description: Explicit vs implicit operator organization affects numerical stability
concept_3.ground_truth: fvm::ddt returns matrix, fvc::ddt returns field (difference in fvmDdt.H vs fvcDdt.H)
concept_3.key_insight: fvm = finite volume matrix (implicit, goes in Ax=b), fvc = finite volume calculus (explicit, calculated directly)
concept_3.lab_relevance: Choosing: fvm::ddt(rho) for unknown vs fvc::div(phi) when phi is known

expansion_term_note.title: Important Note on Phase Change Expansion
expansion_term_note.content: This lab focuses on the CONTINUITY EQUATION (mass conservation). The expansion term for phase change (div(U) = m_dot*(1/rho_v - 1/rho_l)) appears in the PRESSURE EQUATION for incompressible solvers. You will implement pressure-velocity coupling in a later lab (Day 09). For now, focus on understanding the structure: fvm for implicit terms, fvc for explicit terms, and how to add source terms.
expansion_term_note.day_01_reference: Day 01, Section 1.3.3: 'Case 3: Phase Change with Mass Transfer'

deliverables: Test case (forwardStep or cavity) showing convergence
Continuity error plot (Python/Gnuplot from log file)
Lab report (2-3 pages) explaining implementation and results
submission_checklist: Code follows OpenFOAM style guide (capitalization, indentation)
